# W3C conformance across releases

<!-- GENERATED by tools/conformance-timeline/aggregate.py -- do not edit by hand. -->

Every tagged release measured **unmodified** against *today's* W3C suites (reference: `v0.6.0`). See [tools/conformance-timeline](tools/conformance-timeline/README.md) for the method.

![conformance by release](tools/conformance-timeline/conformance-timeline.svg)

тЬЧ hang/OOM crasher ┬╖ тКШ in-scope case never run ┬╖ тЪа documented expected failure тАФ **all three count as not-passing**.

## How a score is computed

The denominator is the set of cases the reference release actually **runs**: today's suite minus the cases the harness skips as *inapplicable* (wrong XML edition, XML 1.1 when helium targets 1.0, dependency gates). Those are not failures and never could be, so charging them to a release would understate it.

| Suite | Applicable | Excluded as inapplicable |
|---|---:|---:|
| XML 1.0/1.1 | 2,001 | 584 |
| XSD 1.0 | 14,399 | 0 |
| XSD 1.1 | 1,049 | 0 |
| XSLT 3.0 | 12,827 | 300 |
| XPath/XQuery (QT3) | 22,328 | 141 |
| XMLDSig 2ed interop | 37 | 0 |
| XMLDSig 1.1 interop | 33 | 0 |
| XMLDSig merlin baseline | 16 | 0 |

Everything else counts against the release: cases it fails, cases it **cannot enumerate** (its compiler chokes before the harness can run them тАФ a hollow dot on the chart), cases it **skips that the reference runs**, cases that **hang or exhaust its memory**, and documented **expected failures**.
